title: Systems and methods for remote demand based data management of clinical locations
abstract: A system and a method for managing one or more clinical protocols across multiple locations, including identify a clinical research location; capture a plurality of types of content from the administrator mobile device; analyze the captured content to determine one or more associated clinical protocols for the clinical research location; automatically select a workflow algorithm for each determined clinical protocol, the workflow algorithm configured to obtain stage based information and workflow items from the data store; and enable a mobile control interface for each determined clinical protocol, wherein the mobile control interface comprises monitoring of the stage based information and management of the workflow items associated with the clinical research location.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A system for managing one or more clinical protocols across multiple locations, comprising: a processor; a data store configured to store workflow items with information for clinical trials employing the one or more clinical protocols; a memory communicatively couple with the processor, the memory configured to store instructions, the instruction configured to cause the processor to: identify a clinical research location; capture a plurality of types of content from the administrator mobile device; analyze the captured content to determine one or more associated clinical protocols for the clinical research location; automatically select a workflow algorithm for each determined clinical protocol, the workflow algorithm configured to obtain stage based information and workflow items from the data store; and enable a mobile control interface for each determined clinical protocol, wherein the mobile control interface comprises monitoring of the stage based information and management of the workflow items associated with the clinical research location. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the mobile control interface comprises contextual messaging of devices associated with each of the clinical protocols, wherein devices associated with each clinical protocol comprise at least one of participant devices, practitioner devices, or facility based systems. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the contextual messaging is based on the participant devices or the practitioner devices detected within a radius of the clinical research location. 4. The system of claim 2, wherein the contextual messaging is in view of the stage based information of the clinical protocol. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the mobile control interface for each determined clinical protocol presents workflow items in view of the stage based information. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein a workflow item in view of the stage based information are associated with a workflow task, the workflow task comprising at least one of acknowledgement, modification, signature, or assignment. 7. The system of claim 5, wherein one or more of the presented workflow items can be sent via a contextual message, using the mobile control interface, to one or more devices associated with the clinical protocols 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the mobile control interface further comprises tracking of protocol activities for participants associated with the clinical research location for each clinical protocol. 9. The system of claim 1, wherein the mobile control interface further comprises analytics for each clinical protocol across the multiple locations. 10. The system of claim 1, wherein the workflow items comprises one or more of documents, certificates, videos, training materials, protocol material, licenses, sharing components, time cards, expense forms, trip reports, locations, contact information, to do lists, calendar information, alerts, and notifications. 11. A method, comprising: identifying a clinical research location; capturing a plurality of types of content from the administrator mobile device; analyzing the captured content to determine one or more associated clinical protocols for the clinical research location; automatically selecting a workflow algorithm for each determined clinical protocol, the workflow algorithm configured to obtain stage based information and workflow items from the data store; and enabling a mobile control interface for each determined clinical protocol, wherein the mobile control interface comprises monitoring of the stage based information and management of the workflow items associated with the clinical research location. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the mobile control interface comprises contextual messaging of devices associated with each of the clinical protocols, wherein devices associated with each clinical protocol comprise at least one of participant devices, practitioner devices, or facility based systems. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the contextual messaging is based on the participant devices or the practitioner devices detected within a radius of the clinical research location. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the contextual messaging is in view of the stage based information of the clinical protocol. 15. The method of claim 11, wherein the mobile control interface for each determined clinical protocol presents workflow items in view of the stage based information. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein a workflow item in view of the stage based information are associated with a workflow task, the workflow task comprising at least one of acknowledgement, modification, signature, or assignment. 17. The method of claim 15, wherein one or more of the presented workflow items can be sent via a contextual message, using the mobile control interface, to one or more devices associated with the clinical protocols 18. The method of claim 11, wherein the mobile control interface further comprises tracking of protocol activities for participants associated with the clinical research location for each clinical protocol. 19. The method of claim 11, wherein the mobile control interface further comprises analytics for each clinical protocol across the multiple locations. 20. The method of claim 11, wherein the workflow items comprises one or more of documents, certificates, videos, training materials, protocol material, licenses, sharing components, time cards, expense forms, trip reports, locations, contact information, to do lists, calendar information, alerts, and notifications.
